journal_entry.description: Discounted non-interest-bearing note at bank as collateralized borrowing
journal_entry.rationale: Management has determined this is a collateralized borrowing (not a sale), so the note receivable remains on the books. The bank advanced cash based on the present value of the note's face value for the remaining 40 days at 15% annual discount rate. Discount = $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,643.84 = $98,356.16. The discount charge is recorded as interest expense. Cash increases (asset), loan payable increases (liability), and interest expense increases.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Collateralized borrowing treatment: note receivable stays on books, bank loan recorded at face value ($100k), discount charge ($1,643.84) is interest expense, cash received is net proceeds ($98,356.16). Debit: 1 asset increase (cash) + 1 expense increase (interest). Credit: 1 liability increase (loan payable).
